campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from the Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships in the city. They also discussed extending a temporary rule (Ordinance 2326) and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships
Extension of temporary Ordinance 2326
Hamilton Avenue bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17
Grant application for bridge planning and feasibility study

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide how to distribute grant money to local organizations and events. They approved which groups would receive funding, how much each group would get, and made some updates to the rules for community events and street closures.
keyTopics: Approved grant recipients and dollar amounts for secured funding
Decided on Competitive Event Grant Program budget for the fiscal year
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grant applications

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting from March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents could watch the proceedings through multiple channels including cable TV, online streaming, and phone call-in options. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages to make it accessible to the community.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translations available in 50+ languages
Meeting accessible via phone dial-in for residents without internet
No public comment period noted for this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. The next zoning hearing will take place instead on April 8, 2026. If you had plans to attend or submit concerns about zoning matters, you'll need to wait until the April date.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning review process

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for community development grants that help pay for housing and social services projects in Sunnyvale. The commission approved previous meeting notes and heard presentations from organizations seeking money to support local housing and human services initiatives.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications being reviewed
Housing and social services projects seeking city support
Approval of February 25 commission meeting minutes
Local organizations pitching for development funding

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses succeed by launching a program to get unregistered businesses to obtain licenses, reaching out to shopping centers, and promoting local shopping through summer events. They also discussed ways the city can partner with private companies to sponsor events and offer incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping unregistered businesses get licensed
Outreach to shopping centers to boost local business participation
Summer Shop Local campaign to encourage residents to buy from Cupertino businesses
Public-private partnerships to fund city events
Tax breaks and incentives to attract new retail and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting where they addressed several postponed items from previous meetings. They voted on a plan to remove weeds from properties around the city as part of a county program, and they opened up discussion about how federal housing grant money will be spent in the coming year.
keyTopics: Weed removal program for overgrown properties
Federal housing grants for 2026-2027
Multiple agenda items postponed to future meetings
Public comment period on housing funding plans

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to recognize Women's History Month and approve two main agreements. The council authorized a disaster relief partnership with the American Red Cross to use city facilities when emergencies occur, and approved a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector to provide email security and backup services for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
